extra.reasoning: The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on all reachable executions. Analysis: (1) sent becomes true ONLY via t2, whose guard requires `not opted_out`; no other transition sets sent:=true. (2) opted_out becomes true ONLY via t1, and no transition ever sets opted_out:=false, so opted_out is monotonic (sticky once true). (3) Crucially, t1 atomically sets BOTH opted_out:=true AND sent:=false in a single transition, so there is no intermediate state with opted_out=true and sent=true at the moment opted_out flips. (4) Once opted_out=true, every applicable event keeps sent false: send_attempt fires t3 (opted_out true) which sets sent:=false; t2 is disabled (guard not opted_out fails); tick (t4) sets sent:=false; optout (t1) sets sent:=false. Therefore every reachable state with opted_out=true has sent=false. The property cannot be violated within any execution length.
